content:
# Internal: Debit Card User Tool Docs

## Debit Card Verification Aids
Use these user-side tools only inside debit-card or electronic-fund-transfer error-resolution conversations. They are not assistant-operated bank systems; they are prompts granted to the simulated customer when the workflow needs customer-provided facts.

1. `get_debit_card_last_4_digits(card_id: string)` retrieves the last four digits for the supplied debit-card identifier. The required parameter is `card_id`.
2. `provide_written_dispute_statement(account_id: string, confirm_written_statement: boolean)` records the customer's confirmation that the conversation can be used as the written statement for provisional-credit eligibility. Both parameters are required.

## Granting The Customer Tool
Grant a user-side tool when the dispute path requires the customer to supply the card identifier or written statement. `give_discoverable_user_tool` returns the exact tool name, full parameter schema, and any prefilled arguments. Tell the customer to call the permanent `call_discoverable_user_tool` wrapper with that exact `discoverable_tool_name` and a JSON `arguments` object containing every required parameter. After granting it, wait for the customer-side result and use that result in the intake record or eligibility analysis. Do not claim that Northstar Bank has called the tool on the customer's behalf.

If the customer has already supplied the needed card detail or statement in conversation, do not ask again unless the dispute workflow still requires the customer-side confirmation. See "Internal: Debit Card Dispute Intake Requirements" for the intake facts that must be gathered before a dispute can proceed.

## Tool Names For This Workflow
The exact user-side tool names available for this article are:

- `get_debit_card_last_4_digits`
- `provide_written_dispute_statement`

Use these names exactly as written. For provisional-credit handling after a written statement is available, see "Debit Card Provisional Credit Policy".
title: Internal: Debit Card User Tool Docs
